title: Body-size cap before activity parsing prevents JSON bomb DoS
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:57-58 and src/server.rs:900

FINDING: The 256KB DefaultBodyLimit is applied at the router layer (line 58), before any handler executes. This means serde_json::from_str is only called on bodies that are already bounded, preventing a maliciously large JSON body from consuming memory during deserialization. The serde_json 128-level recursion limit comment on line 900 correctly notes the additional protection against deeply-nested structures.

The defense is layered in the right order — size before parse — and should be preserved. If someone proposes moving the body limit inside individual handlers (for 'flexibility'), that would create a window where unbounded bodies are read into memory before the limit is applied.
close_reason: Acknowledged: body size cap design is correct
